original_abstract: This article examines how key dimensions of international educational integration influence the reform of national vocational and higher education systems. We identify and analyze these integrative processes across several critical strata: statutory regulation, the overarching mission of higher education, organizational restructuring, quality assurance protocols, and the management of curricular content. Furthermore, the study evaluates the competitive landscape of the Russian educational market, weighing the specific benefits and risks associated with system-wide reforms. We also assess the broader economic and social impacts of hosting international students within Russian universities. These findings offer practical utility for departments of international cooperation seeking to align national schools with a unified global educational space. Additionally, the research serves as a resource for academic leadership in developing strategies and teaching aids centered on the challenges of reforming professional education within the framework of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes.
humanized_abstract: The article explores how different factors of international educational integration affect national reforms of vocational and higher education. The integrative processes of educational integration are described on different levels (from legislative regulation to the structure of higher education institutions, procedures of accreditation and quality assurance of educational activities and the management of educational material). The competitiveness of the educational market in Russia is estimated together with the positive and negative factors of the introduction of each of the considered reform components. The impact of the admission of international students to Russian universities is also analyzed. The relevance of the research results for departments of international cooperation of educational schools aiming to synchronise the development of Russian educational schools with the creation of a single global educational space is stated. Recommendations for academic management for designing strategies and educational materials for modernising professional education in the context of realisation of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes are also provided.
